user.title: Solar Forecast & Global Switch
user.description:
Enter the sensor prefix for your solar forecast sensors. These sensors should have suffixes like _today, _tomorrow, _d2 etc. and provide a 'watts' attribute.

Optionally set a global switch. This is used as a fallback for inverters that don't have their own switch configured. Useful when you have one central switch controlling multiple inverters.

data_description.global_on_entity: Optional. Used as fallback for inverters without their own turn-on entity.
data_description.global_off_entity: Optional. Used as fallback for inverters without their own turn-off entity.

inverter.title: Inverter {inverter_num}
inverter.description: Configure your inverter. Leave the switch fields empty to use the global switch (fallback) configured in the previous step. Fill them in to use a specific switch for this inverter.

data.inverter_power_control_entity: Power control entity (number, for power limiting)

data_description.inverter_on_entity: Optional. Leave empty to use the global switch if configured.
data_description.inverter_off_entity: Optional. Leave empty to use the global switch if configured.
data_description.inverter_power_control_entity: Optional. Only needed for inverters that support power limiting (modulatable).

energy_core.title: Energy Core
energy_core.description: Select the Energy Core instance to use.

error.no_control_entity: At least one control entity is required (own switch or power control, or set a global switch in the previous step)

abort.energy_core_required: Energy Core integration must be installed and configured first

edit_global_switch.title: Global Switch
edit_global_switch.description: Set a global switch used as fallback for inverters without their own switch.
